info.title: Multi-Agent API
info.description: API with listing and markets agents

get.description:
Up to `limit` map pins: one row per building/property.

**Bounding box mode:** pass `west`, `south`, `east`, `north` (visible map extent). Listings are
filtered to points inside the box; results are ordered by distance to the box center.

**Radius mode (legacy):** pass `latitude`, `longitude`, and `radius_miles`. When any inventory
exists in that radius, results stay inside the circle; otherwise nearest buildings globally.

Requires PostgreSQL, DATABASE_URL, LISTINGS_TABLE_NAME, latitude/longitude columns.

parameters.name: min_rent

schema.description: Minimum monthly rent (USD). Rows with no rent data are kept.
schema.title: Min Rent

parameters.description: Minimum monthly rent (USD). Rows with no rent data are kept.

schema.description: Maximum monthly rent (USD). Rows with no rent data are kept.
schema.title: Max Rent

parameters.description: Maximum monthly rent (USD). Rows with no rent data are kept.

get.description: Return all distinct property keys for sitemap generation.

parameters.name: zip

schema.description: US ZIP; first 5 digits used (supports ZIP+4).
schema.title: Zip

parameters.description: US ZIP; first 5 digits used (supports ZIP+4).

parameters.name: address

schema.description: Deprecated fallback: extract ZIP from free-text only if zip/city/state are missing.
schema.title: Address

parameters.description: Deprecated fallback: extract ZIP from free-text only if zip/city/state are missing.

get.description:
Rent distribution for the listings inside a map bounding box or a
city/state, shaped so the PriceRangeFilter UI can draw a histogram and
anchor the dual-handle slider to real data. Buckets are dynamic: the
scale runs between the 1st and 99th percentile of observed rent so a
single outlier can't flatten the chart.

Return the N nearest transit stations to a given point, with straight-line
walk times at 3 mph + 20% detour overhead. Used for filtering/labeling
JC listings by PATH/HBLR/ferry proximity.

parameters.name: systems

schema.description: Comma-separated list of transit systems to consider (e.g. 'path,hblr,ferry'). Omit for all systems.
schema.title: Systems

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of transit systems to consider (e.g. 'path,hblr,ferry'). Omit for all systems.

get.description:
Return transit stations for map overlays. All four bbox params must
be provided together or all omitted. With no bbox, returns every
seeded station (currently ~490 across PATH/HBLR/ferry/NYC subway).

parameters.name: systems

schema.description: Comma-separated systems to include (e.g. 'path,hblr,ferry,nyc_subway'). Omit for all systems.
schema.title: Systems

parameters.description: Comma-separated systems to include (e.g. 'path,hblr,ferry,nyc_subway'). Omit for all systems.

post.description:
Fire-and-forget endpoint: parse + geocode + dedupe-check + create in one call.

Returns one of three outcomes so the client can show a terminal state per
queued paste without extra round trips:
  * listing populated → saved
  * dedupe_matches non-empty → user must confirm (retry with force=True)
  * parse_error populated → couldn't recover an address / coords

get.description: Public endpoint — no auth required. Returns vendors available in the given state.

get.description: Backward-compat endpoint for photos uploaded to local disk before Azure migration.
List inventory rows with no `listing_amenities` rows (admin). Large tables: COUNT may be slow.

schema.description: When true, count only rows deemed available (availability_status and/or rent).
schema.title: Available Only

parameters.description: When true, count only rows deemed available (availability_status and/or rent).

schema.title: Response List Report Subscriptions Property Manager Report Subscriptions Get

post.description:
Universal upload endpoint for the chat paperclip.

Sniffs content-type / extension and routes to the appropriate ingestion path.
The chat UI stitches the returned `{kind, id, summary, ...}` into the next
user message annotation so the orchestrator can route to the right specialist.

BuildingResolveRequest.title: BuildingResolveRequest
BuildingResolveRequest.description:
Lookup-or-create a building by address + lat/lng.

BBL/BIN is optional (filled in later by the Geosupport ingest job). A client
calling this endpoint with just an address and Mapbox-geocoded lat/lng is
enough to give us a canonical building row for review attribution.

llamaCloudPipeline.description: The selected LlamaCloud pipeline to use for the chat

ChatRequest.title: ChatRequest
ChatRequest.description: The request to the chat API.

status.title: Status
status.description: One of: verified, failed, declined, revoked

ListingRowPeek.title: ListingRowPeek
ListingRowPeek.description: Up to a few concrete rows for a building_key — use to validate giant property_id buckets.

ListingsPerBuildingProof.title: ListingsPerBuildingProof
ListingsPerBuildingProof.description: SQL proof that row count ÷ distinct buildings matches unit/listing density per property.

The selected LlamaCloud pipeline to use for the chat.
(Only available when the app is configured to use LlamaCloud)

zip.title: Zip
zip.description: 5-digit US ZIP when the filter used postal code.

MetroCoverageEstimate.title: MetroCoverageEstimate
MetroCoverageEstimate.description:
Metro-wide total is not knowable from inventory DB alone; set
`estimated_metro_rental_buildings` in nyc_metro_coverage_context.json (your benchmark).

MetroCoveragePieSlice.title: MetroCoveragePieSlice
MetroCoveragePieSlice.description: Buildings count per slice for market vs platform donut.

total_in_radius.title: Total In Radius
total_in_radius.description: Count of listings matching the query (radius circle or map bbox) before limit.

radius_miles.title: Radius Miles
radius_miles.description: Search radius in miles when using center+radius mode; 0 when using bbox mode.

used_global_nearest_fallback.title: Used Global Nearest Fallback
used_global_nearest_fallback.description: True when nothing matched the radius; properties are the nearest rows with coordinates (any distance) so the map is still usable.

used_bbox.title: Used Bbox
used_bbox.description: True when results were filtered by west/south/east/north bounding box.

stations.title: Stations
stations.description: Nearest stations across requested systems, sorted by walk time ascending.

system.title: System
system.description: path | hblr | nyc_subway | lirr | nj_transit_rail | ferry

walk_minutes.title: Walk Minutes
walk_minutes.description: Estimated straight-line walk time at 3 mph (adds ~20% detour overhead).

ParseUrlRequest.title: ParseUrlRequest
ParseUrlRequest.description:
Accepts either a bare URL or a free-form paste (e.g. a Zillow share message).

If `text` is provided it is preferred: an LLM pulls a URL and address hint
out of it so a share blob like `"269 Terrace Ave #B, Jersey City, NJ 07307
| Zillow https://share.google/…"` can be pasted directly.

PasteCreateRequest.title: PasteCreateRequest
PasteCreateRequest.description: One-shot: parse + geocode + create. For the fire-and-forget paste queue.

PasteCreateResponse.title: PasteCreateResponse
PasteCreateResponse.description: Exactly one of (listing, dedupe_matches, parse_error) is meaningfully populated.

nearest_distance_meters.title: Nearest Distance Meters
nearest_distance_meters.description: Straight-line distance from the query pin to the nearest POI (meters).

index.title: Index
index.description: Bucket index. 1..bucket_count lie within the [p01, p99] range; 0 is the below-range overflow and bucket_count+1 the above-range overflow.

min_rent.title: Min Rent
min_rent.description: Lower edge of this bucket's rent range (USD/month).

max_rent.title: Max Rent
max_rent.description: Upper edge of this bucket's rent range (USD/month).

sample_size.title: Sample Size
sample_size.description: Listings with a usable rent in scope.

bucket_count.title: Bucket Count
bucket_count.description: Number of interior buckets spanning [range_min, range_max].

range_min.title: Range Min
range_min.description: Lower edge of the histogram scale (p01, rounded). Null when no data.

range_max.title: Range Max
range_max.description: Upper edge of the histogram scale (p99, rounded). Null when no data.

listing_url.title: Listing Url
listing_url.description: Original listing page URL scraped from the provider site (if available).

city.title: City
city.description: City or locality from the listings row when present (e.g. units.parquet `city`).

state.title: State
state.description: State / region code from the listings row when present (e.g. units.parquet `state`).

zip_code.title: Zip Code
zip_code.description: Postal code from the listings row when present (e.g. units.parquet `zipcode`).

latitude.title: Latitude
latitude.description: Latitude of the property if available from the query results.

longitude.title: Longitude
longitude.description: Longitude of the property if available from the query results.

rent_range.title: Rent Range
rent_range.description: rent range from the units available with currency, e.g., $2000-$2500

bedroom_range.title: Bedroom Range
bedroom_range.description: bedroom range from the units available, e.g., 1-3 bedrooms

images_urls.title: Images Urls
images_urls.description: list of image URLs of the property

main_amenities.title: Main Amenities
main_amenities.description:
List of the four main amenities of the property, which should include things related to:
            - pool
            - Gym / Fitness center
            - Parking / Garage
            - Policies, e.g., pet friendly
            - Location features, e.g., waterfront, beach access
            - Building features, e.g., elevator, doorman

            Maximum of four items.
            It's important to keep the items concise and avoid full sentences.

amenities.title: Amenities
amenities.description: list of amenities available in the property

match_reason.title: Match Reason
match_reason.description: One short sentence (max ~200 chars) explaining why this listing matches the user's search (e.g. name/amenities mention 'park', proximity, filters applied).

validation_status.title: Validation Status
validation_status.description: Semantic/LLM validation state for dynamic UI: candidate|validating|confirmed|rejected.

validation_explanation.title: Validation Explanation
validation_explanation.description: Short explanation of why the listing is relevant to the current query.

concessions.title: Concessions
concessions.description: Promo / special-offer lines when present: listing_amenities rows, other special columns, or amenity text that matches typical concession patterns (e.g. '1 month free').

available_date.title: Available Date
available_date.description: Earliest move-in / availability label when present in source data.

message.title: Message
message.default: Check your email to verify your account.

unlisted_market_share_pct.title: Unlisted Market Share Pct
unlisted_market_share_pct.description: Assumed share of rental units not represented in the listing sample (0–100).

assumed_unlisted_vacancy_pct.title: Assumed Unlisted Vacancy Pct
assumed_unlisted_vacancy_pct.description: Vacancy assumed for unlisted units (mostly occupied).

SupplyPressure.title: SupplyPressure
SupplyPressure.description:
Vacancy from listing availability, plus a market-wide estimate.

Scrapers only see units that were listed at least once, so ``total_units`` understates
the true stock. ``vacancy_rate_pct`` scales the denominator and assumes mostly-occupied
unlisted units; ``listing_sample_vacancy_rate_pct`` is the raw listing-only ratio.

system.title: System
system.description: path | hblr | nyc_subway | lirr | nj_transit_rail | ferry

stations.title: Stations
stations.description: Transit stations in the requested bbox (or all, if unfiltered).
